calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gener Benitez for and with Maria O'Toole (Trustee). Patrick Hill from Legal Assistance for the Elderly for Teresa Chua.

Mr. Hill stated that there was a global settlement agreement, that withdrawal of the objection was filed electronically last night, and that the property is scheduled to be repaired and sold, and then distributed. Mr. Hill also stated that Ms. Chua has no objection to the second accounting, that the second account can be approved, asked the Court to retain jurisdiction pursuant to 664.6 to enforce the terms of the settlement agreement and requested an accounting for final distribution after the house is sold. The Court suggested Mr. Benitez informally account for the Trust proceeds ($69,000.00) and the sale proceeds. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Continued To Aug-21-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Declaratory Releif By The Trustee, Maria O'Toole, That There Is No Oral Trust Giving T. Chua A Life Estate, For The Life Of T. Chua, In The 6th Ave Property.
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Continued To Aug-21-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gener Benitez for and with Maria O'Toole. Patrick Hill for and with Teresa Chua. Jerome Schweich in association with Attorney of record specially appearing for Mr. Drohan for and with Teresa Chua.

The Court did not rule on Mr. Benitez' objection to Mr. Schweich's appearance, the issue of confidentiality of information regarding mediation, jursidiction or the request for consolidation with civil case number CGC-13-531198. The parties agreed to meet with the Court's Settlement Officer Pang Ly. The Court will have Ms. Ly contact the Parties. Ms. Ly is to address the issue of association of Counsel. Mr. Schweich stated that he will participate in settlement discussion. The Petition for Declaratory Relief by The Trustee, etc. and the accounting petition were continued to August 21, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Mann.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2
Off Calendar May-14-2013 Continued To Jun-18-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gener Benitez for and with Maria O'Toole. Patrick Hill for and with Francis Chua.

Mr. Benitez objected to Mr. Hill's request for a continuance. The Court raised the issue of the payee for the VA payment, questioned the expense for the lap top, indicated that it would be helpful for the Court to know how the VA payments were spent, and stated that the Attorney's fees regarding removal may need to be deferred as it is on appeal. The Court encouraged the Parties to consider mediation, noted the expense of litigation and stated that time sheets will help the Court adjudicate the Attorney's fees. The accounting petition was continued to June 18, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court would like a further declaration regarding Mr. Benitez' fees, information on how the VA payments were spent and status on the appeal by June 11, 2013.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Sarah Brann (CSR: 3887. Tel: 415-357-4300) Clerk: Nancy Bautista/ Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-18-2013 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Removal Of Trustee, Account, And Appointment Of Successor, And For Preliminary Injuction.
Off Calendar Mar-14-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gener Benitez for and with Maria O'Toole. William Lynn for and with Teresa Chua.

The Court stated that the original bank statements or brokerage statements were needed for the accounting petition. Mr. Benitez stated that the statements were filed with the original account on 1/26/11, that he has the supplement addressing the fair market value of the assets on hand, and that he also has the list of the vested contingent beneficiaries with him today. The Court reviewed the supplement and directed Counsel to file it today. Mr. Lynn presented argument regarding Ms. O'Toole's authority to make distributions during the account period, stated that the Co-Trustee's need to act together, and that Ms. O'Toole directly disobeyed the Trust terms. Mr. Benitez argued that Probate Code 15621 actually applies here not Probate Code 15620. Additional argument held. The Court took the accounting petition under submission, stated that it would un-submit the matter if it couldn't locate the original statements, and that Counsel would hear then from the Examiner. Both parties argued their respective positions in regard to the removal petition. Mr. Lynn requested the Court provide him with its' rulings in regard to their objections previously made in his 1/12/11 filing. The Court ruled that the Petitioner (Ms. Chua) had not met her burden of proof, that there was no basis for the petition, that the Court doesn't need to rule on the evidentiary objections, and therefore the petition is denied. Mr. Benitez is to prepare the order and as part of the denial order is to include the language that the Petitioner did not meet her burden of proof and therefore the Court doesn't need to rule on the evidentiary objections. The petition for removal was denied and the accounting petition was taken under submission. The Court stated that it would retain jurisdiction over the trust, and that the second accounting is due two weeks before March 18, 2013 which will be for the account period from 12/28/10 to 12/27/12. The Court authorized Mr. Benitez to use the bank statement ending the closest to the 12/27/12 date for second account.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting By Maria O'Toole
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Off Calendar

Petition For Removal Of Trustee, Account, And Appointment Of Successor, And For Preliminary Injuction.
Off Calendar Jan-24-2011 Continued To Mar-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gene Benitez for and with Maris O'Toole. William Lynn and James Oddie for and with Teresa Chua.

Mr. Benitez' Client was not willing to participate in mediation or consider having a neutral Trustee. Mr. Lynn stated that they were not satisifed with the accounting and spoke of obtaining a declaration from Dr. Kong regarding capacity for the period of 2002 through 2005. The Court stated that it wanted the accounting to be properly filed and noticed, and that it would not review the accounting that had already been filed. Mr. Benitez is to file the accounting by February 2, 2011 with a hearing date of March 14, 2011. Status of filing of said accounting is set for February 7, 2011. Objections are to be filed by February 25, 2011. Replies if any are to be filed by March 4, 2011. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. was continued to March 14, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Removal Of Trustee, Account, And Appointment Of Successor, And For Preliminary Injuction.
Off Calendar Jan-18-2011 Continued To Jan-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing Re: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-20-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Lynn for and with Teresa Chua (Petitioner). Gener Benitez for and with Maria O'Toole (Respondent).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Settlor's incapacity, whether the Settlor would or would not recover, the house being sold, one Co-Trustee having power to act when the other is not able to act, the Petitioner having/not having standing, and the Petitioner having the right/not having the right to reside in the home rent free. The Court denied the preliminary injunction. Mr. Benitez is to prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Ex Parte Application For Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Nov-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
